mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates applied to future cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the consumer price index remains elevated, stagflationary pressures force monetary tightening and compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns dominate in more than 60 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction and weak near-term earnings expectations, driving negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances following a month-over-month industrial production decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in retail sales below the three-month moving average signals weakening consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue forecasts and causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative direction with greater than 50 percent accuracy over rolling two-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than the consumer price index, real interest rates become restrictive, slowing economic activity and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Real rate compression shows no predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.02, or positive returns occur in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, supporting positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observed cases over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts signals strong construction sector demand and positive household wealth effects, driving positive short-term market returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts surges fail to predict positive 5-day returns with accuracy exceeding 50 percent over a three-year walk-forward test.
